title: Public opinion trajectory regarding the proposed US ban of TikTok in 2020

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Baseline sentiment prior to Secretary Pompeo's public comments
Estimated: Post-Pompeo announcement of potential ban
Estimated: Following Trump's executive order threats
Estimated: Reflecting peak media coverage of Oracle/Microsoft acquisition bids
Estimated: Stabilization after court injunctions delayed the ban

events.description: Secretary of State Mike Pompeo states the US is looking at banning TikTok over data privacy concerns.
President Trump signs executive order prohibiting transactions with ByteDance after 45 days.
Oracle selected as TikTok's US technology partner, complicating the ban narrative.

agents.name: Donald Trump
agents.description: US President advocating for a total ban citing national security.

agents.name: Mike Pompeo
agents.description: Secretary of State driving the anti-TikTok rhetoric.

agents.name: ByteDance
agents.description: Parent company of TikTok lobbying against the ban.

agents.name: TikTok Creators
agents.description: Large community of influencers opposing the ban due to economic impact.

agents.name: Tech Policy Analysts
agents.description: Experts debating the technical feasibility and necessity of the ban.

agents.name: General Public
agents.description: The broader US population with varying levels of digital literacy.

notes: Pro-side is defined as supporting a ban or strict government restrictions on TikTok; Against-side is defined as opposing a ban or favoring continued operation. Data quality is high for the mid-point (Pew survey), while early and late rounds are estimated based on news cycles and political momentum.
